rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DHIREN DWIJEN DESAI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Settlement, For Allowance Of Statutory Commissions And Attorneys Fees And Decree Of Distribution Under Intestate Succession
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues posted for the hearing on 10/22/24 still have not been addressed. The Court to determine if the administrator should be suspended or removed. (Yet another incorrect "Corrected Final Supplemental" I&A has been filed, showing non-cash assets that should be appraised by the probate referee appraised by the administrator as "cash" and ostensibly verified on 2/8/23.) Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VERNON EUGENE CASTAGNETTO
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although paragraph 2 of the supplement filed on 3/13/25 does not make grammatical sense. Status of filing a petition for final distribution or a second report of status of administration with a petition requesting additional time must be filed at least two weeks before 12/23/25. Appearance will be required on that date if no petition is filed. (NB: It is not necessary to narrate how the petitioner will cure the defects with his petition or notice; the additional information should just be provided (in a supplement), or incorrect allegations should be corrected (in an amendment), or a proof of service should just be filed to show that full, correct notice was given.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ENRIGHT FAMILY REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Accounting, Demand For Distribution, For Breach Of Trust, Removal And Appointment Of Independent Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required to update the Court, as no joint status report has been filed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ENRIGHT FAMILY REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Demand Of Property Tax Reimbursement
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, despite the following outstanding issues: 1) Direct notice to Lusyana Enright was mailed to her attorney's address, rather than to her place of business or residence. See Probate Code sections 1215(a)(3) & 17203(a); see also CRC 7.51(a)(2). 2) Lusyana Enright's address set forth in the supplement is her attorney's address, rather than her place of business or residence. 3) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) was not timely served with notice of the previous (03/19/2025) hearing. A copy was not attached to the proof of service of notice of this hearing. Note: A copy of the proposed order must be served on each person entitled to notice if a matter becomes contested. Proof of service must be filed. See LRSF 14.7(A)(4). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL RICHAD NAZZAL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/17/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) What happened to the numerous other securities if the executor took no actions without court supervision or without sending a notice of proposed action but only two remain on hand? (2) Does the executor have a valid Firearm Safety Certificate and did he submit a Report of Operation of Law to the Department of Justice within 30 days of taking possession of the firearms? [California Penal Code sections 16990 and 27920.] (3) Proposed distribution must show specifically how assets are to be distributed to each beneficiary, including the net amount of cash and the number of shares of each security. (How will the three beneficiaries jointly own the vehicle and the firearms...?) Please provide a (revised) proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/3/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (It would have been helpful if a courtesy copy of the petition had been provided, as required by LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. It would furthermore have been helpful if the petition had mentioned the prior order determining what was to happen to the remaining 1% interest in the estate; that order was only discovered after the examiner reviewed the entire register of actions.) NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANITA M. GARCIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/11/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The filed "original" will appears to be a photocopy on which someone has traced the signatures in ink. (2) If the petitioner is also the trustee of the sole beneficiary's trust, which might or might not be the ultimate beneficiary of the assets distributable to him from this estate, the beneficiaries of his trust must be identified and must receive (or waive) notice. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/28/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RON G. VEST
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/17/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) has not yet been filed. [Probate Code Section 8404(b).] Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (2) No supplement has been filed yet to address incomplete item 3e(1) of the petition or the erroneous newspaper noted in item 1 of the petition. (3) Nothing has been filed yet to substantiate how Angela Joy Vest is not entitled to take pursuant Probate Code Section 250. (4) Lipton Mah is one of the two witnesses to the will and also a beneficiary. [Probate Code Section 6112.] Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/3/25. It would be very helpful if a copy of the petition were also provided to the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Still no courtesy copy of any of the filed papers has been provided, which hampers review. No notice is required for a continued hearing unless ordered by the Court. [Probate Code Section 1205.]) NB: Courtesy copies are FILE STAMPED, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.]
